00029 
00030 /*
00031  * Sample/test code for running a user program.  You can use this for
00032  * reference when implementing the execv() system call. Remember though
00033  * that execv() needs to do more than this function does.
00034  */
00035 
00036 #include <types.h>
00037 #include <kern/errno.h>
00038 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00039 #include <lib.h>
00040 #include <thread.h>
00041 #include <current.h>
00042 #include <addrspace.h>
00043 #include <vm.h>
00044 #include <vfs.h>
00045 #include <syscall.h>
00046 #include <test.h>
00047 #include <copyinout.h>
00048 
00049 /*
00050  * Load program "progname" and start running it in usermode.
00051  * Does not return except on error.
00052  *
00053  * Calls vfs_open on progname and thus may destroy it.
00054  */
00055 int
00056 runprogram(char *progname, int nargs, char **args)
00057 {
00058         struct vnode *v;
00059         vaddr_t entrypoint, stackptr;
00060         int result;
00061         char **p_args;          /* kernel buffer */
00062         char **new_args;        /* args in user space */
00063         int i, len, total_len;
00064 
00065         /* Copy args from command line to p_args */
00066         p_args = (char **)kmalloc(sizeof(char *) * (nargs + 1));
00067         for (i = 0; i < nargs; i++) {
00068                 p_args[i] = kstrdup(args[i]);
00069         }
00070         p_args[nargs] = NULL;
00071 
00072         /* Open the file. */
00073         result = vfs_open(progname, O_RDONLY, 0, &v);
00074         if (result) {
00075                 return result;
00076         }
00077 
00078 
00079         /* We should be a new thread. */
00080         KASSERT(curthread->t_addrspace == NULL);
00081 
00082 
00083         /* Create a new address space. */
00084         curthread->t_addrspace = as_create();
00085         if (curthread->t_addrspace==NULL) {
00086                 vfs_close(v);
00087                 return ENOMEM;
00088         }
00089 
00090         /* Activate it. */
00091         as_activate(curthread->t_addrspace);
00092 
00093 
00094         /* Load the executable. */
00095         result = load_elf(v, &entrypoint);
00096         if (result) {
00097                 /* thread_exit destroys curthread->t_addrspace */
00098                 vfs_close(v);
00099                 return result;
00100         }
00101 
00102 
00103         /* Done with the file now. */
00104         vfs_close(v);
00105 
00106 
00107         /* Define the user stack in the address space */
00108         result = as_define_stack(curthread->t_addrspace, &stackptr);
00109         if (result) {
00110                 /* thread_exit destroys curthread->t_addrspace */
00111                 return result;
00112         }
00113 
00114 
00115         /* Calculate total length of memory buffer for ensure that
00116          * data is properly aligned. They must start
00117          * at an address that is divisible by 4.
00118          */
00119         total_len = 0;
00120         for (i = 0; i < nargs; i++) {
00121                 len = ((int)strlen(p_args[i]) / 4 + 1) * 4;
00122                         total_len += len;
00123         }
00124 
00125         /* Address of the args in user space */
00126         new_args = (char**)(stackptr - total_len - (nargs + 1)*4 - STACK_OFFSET);
00127 
00128         new_args[nargs] = NULL;
00129 
00130         len = 0;
00131 
00132         /* Copy the args aligned in user space */
00133         for (i = nargs - 1; i > -1; i--) {
00134                 len += ((int)strlen(p_args[i]) / 4 + 1) * 4;
00135                 new_args[i] = (char*)(stackptr - len - (STACK_OFFSET));
00136                 copyout(p_args[i], (userptr_t)new_args[i], strlen(p_args[i]));
00137         }
00138 
00139         /* Free kernel buffer memory */
00140         for (i = 0; i < nargs; i++) {
00141                 kfree(p_args[i]);
00142         }
00143         kfree(p_args);
00144 
00145         /* Warp to user mode. */
00146         enter_new_process(nargs/*argc*/,  (userptr_t)new_args/*userspace addr of argv*/,
00147                           stackptr, entrypoint);
00148         
00149         /* enter_new_process does not return. */
00150         panic("enter_new_process returned\n");
00151         return EINVAL;
00152 }
00153
